behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at service↔storage handoff (summary write must query same-round findings). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: verdict source is stored FAIL/PASS flags, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: single gate decision. Adversarial critique: if implemented as one pure function over in-memory findings, Unit could be enough; retained Integration because behavior text explicitly says "stored" and "same round" consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Use real repository/storage modules (e.g., test DB). Persist same-round findings including one FAIL, attempt to persist PASS summary, assert write is rejected and rejection reason references conflicting stored dimension verdict.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is wrong data source selection across modules (gate using summary text instead of findings store). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed. LLM-DEP: deterministic computation over stored records. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain required. Adversarial critique: if gate function already receives both objects and picks one field, Unit might catch it; kept Integration because behavior is specifically about reading from persistence boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Seed storage with findings containing FAIL and a contradictory manual PASS summary text. Run gate computation through service layer and assert final verdict is non-PASS based on stored per-dimension findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: enforcement and audit emission cross request parsing, policy check, and audit store. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network requirement. LLM-DEP: independent of model output content. MULTI-STEP: single decision + audit side effect. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if audit sink is just a function call; kept Integration because behavior requires durable "auditable warning record" generation.
behaviors.test_description: Call summary-save path without override flag and assert override is rejected. Call again with flag and assert save succeeds only with a persisted warning/audit record linked to round and actor.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is stale-state comparison across commit metadata and review-round metadata. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with stored commit SHAs/timestamps; no real git subprocess required by behavior text. LLM-DEP: checks freshness metadata, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: still one gating rule. Rejection-gate for higher levels: behavior says "requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit" (metadata ordering), not "requires validating model output quality." Adversarial critique: if production derives latest commit only via real git commands, System might be needed.
behaviors.test_description: Persist fix commit A, then old review round tied to pre-A commit. Attempt PASS finalization and assert blocked. Persist new review round tied to post-A commit and assert gate can proceed.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure emerges across sequenced stages (fix commit → agent round trigger → stored results → final gate). LLM-DEP: involves real review-agent run path; a stubbed constant round can hide orchestration bugs around actual run completion/freshness. MULTI-STEP: multiple staged decisions with at least one agentic step, so Workflow over Agentic. Adversarial critique: if system only checks presence of a new round ID, Integration could catch it; retained Workflow because behavior explicitly requires the live loop and actual fresh agent round execution on updated diff.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end live loop: create failing round, apply fix commit, try finalize without rerun (must block), then execute a real review-agent round on updated diff and verify PASS can only finalize after that round is recorded as fresh.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires coordination between round-counter policy and stored findings severity. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: decision is based on stored MUST-FIX flags, not real-time model quality. MULTI-STEP: policy evaluation at terminal state, not full live pipeline. Adversarial critique: Unit possible if all inputs already assembled into one object; kept Integration because behavior depends on "stored results" and exhaustion state from separate modules.
behaviors.test_description: Seed storage with unresolved MUST-FIX findings and round_count == max_rounds. Execute termination/gate service and assert final status is escalation and PASS is forbidden.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug can hide in cross-round orchestration (state carryover, decrementing rounds, terminal escalation emission). LLM-DEP: each round is agent-mediated in the live workflow, and correctness depends on sequencing of those rounds. MULTI-STEP: explicit multi-round agent pipeline, so Workflow is required by definition. Rejection-gate for lower levels: behavior text says "live multi-round review workflow," disqualifying single-step Integration. Adversarial critique: if round outcomes are fully precomputed fixtures and only terminal reducer is tested, Integration might suffice; retained Workflow because behavior explicitly targets live multi-round execution.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live multi-round run up to max rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings persisting; assert PASS remains blocked at exhaustion and escalation event/record is emitted exactly at terminal transition.
